title: medicago tornata subsp. helix
intro: Medicago tornata subsp. helix, also known as the false clover, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Medicago tornata subsp. helix include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support urinary tract health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
